justifications.completeness: Extraction detects one pedestrian and two vehicles while ground truth contains two pedestrians and five vehicles, and track ids do not match, so many objects are missing or mislabeled.
justifications.semantic_richness: Attributes like atomic actions, context, age for pedestrians and motion status plus trunk/door states for vehicles are provided, showing good attribute coverage despite being applied to few objects and with id mismatches.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are present and informative but reference incorrect track ids and are sparse relative to the ground truth vehicle-pedestrian relations, reducing their correctness.
justifications.overall: Moderate semantic detail is supplied but low recall of entities and incorrect id mapping significantly limit reliability.

justifications.completeness: Many ground-truth objects are missing or mismatched: both annotated pedestrians (IDs 0 and 3) are not correctly represented and multiple ground-truth vehicles (IDs 1, 4, 5) are omitted or misaligned with extra tracks.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction contains many semantic fields (actions, age, trunk/door states), but several attributes are assigned to incorrect tracks or overconfidently filled where ground truth is unknown, reducing reliability.
justifications.spatial_relations: Several spatial relations are provided, but they are inconsistent with ground-truth identities and contain ambiguous or likely incorrect relations (for example ambiguous ahead/behind and parking/motion assignments).
justifications.overall: Significant ID mismatches and missing objects combined with partially correct attributes yield limited overall usefulness despite some correct elements like a moving truck.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only one pedestrian and two vehicles while the ground truth has four pedestrians and five vehicles, so most objects are missed or misidentified.
justifications.semantic_richness: The VLM supplies many attribute fields (actions, age, vehicle motion, trunk/door states and spatial relations) indicating reasonable richness, but these are provided for very few objects and some attributes are incorrectly associated.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are present and somewhat descriptive, but several relations conflict with ground-truth identities and many objects have no spatial relations reported.
justifications.overall: Given substantial missed detections and ID/type mismatches despite some detailed attributes, the overall extraction quality is low.

justifications.completeness: Many ground truth persons and vehicles are missing or mislabeled; only one pedestrian and two vehicles were reported with incorrect track ids.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes multiple attribute types (actions, transport, age, vehicle states) showing good richness, but several attribute values are incorrect or unsupported by the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Reported spatial relations are present but reference nonexistent track ids and are inconsistent with ground truth, reducing their reliability.
justifications.overall: The extraction provides diverse semantic fields but suffers from poor coverage and several incorrect labels, resulting in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures only one pedestrian and two vehicles while the ground truth contains four pedestrians and six vehicles, so the detection is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some semantic attributes (atomic action, age, motion status, trunk/door states) are provided, but many entities and important labels like transporting and complex context for other pedestrians are missing.
justifications.spatial_relations: The reported spatial relations are coarse and assigned to only a few detected objects, with no consistent correspondence to ground truth track IDs or the majority of scene objects.
justifications.overall: Due to large omissions in detected entities and incomplete attribute assignment the extraction is limited in usefulness despite some correct semantic labels.

justifications.completeness: Missed all three pedestrians and detected only two vehicles none of which match the ground-truth vehicle IDs, so recall is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic attributes (type, motion, doors) for two objects but omits pedestrians, many vehicles and finer person attributes, giving low semantic coverage.
justifications.spatial_relations: Gives left/right and ahead relations for its detections which is useful but these relations apply to incorrect or missing objects so spatial accuracy is limited.
justifications.overall: Extraction is inaccurate and incomplete, missing all pedestrians and most vehicles, so overall quality is poor.

justifications.completeness: The model missed all three pedestrians and only reported two vehicle instances that do not match the ground truth IDs, so object coverage is very poor.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes several vehicle attributes and spatial phrases but omits pedestrian attributes and many ground-truth vehicles, and some provided attributes appear incorrect.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial phrases are present but they refer to misidentified objects and do not align with ground-truth identities or full scene context, so spatial accuracy is low.
justifications.overall: Missed pedestrians, misclassification of people as trucks, and incorrect/incomplete vehicle reporting lead to a poor overall extraction.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures only one unlabeled pedestrian and two vehicles while the ground truth lists six pedestrians and seven vehicles, so it misses the majority of objects.
justifications.semantic_richness: It reports some attributes (motion_status, trunk/doors, age) but omits many GT attributes (multiple pedestrian contexts, transporting details) and mislabels object identities, yielding limited richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided relations are present but often incorrect or ambiguous (e.g., a pedestrian labeled on the sidewalk despite GT indicating several pedestrians on the road) and vehicle relations do not correspond to GT vehicle IDs.
justifications.overall: Due to major omissions, incorrect track associations, and several misassigned spatial relations the extraction is poor overall despite some correctly reported attributes.

justifications.completeness: Many ground-truth persons and vehicles are missing or have mismatched track IDs, so the extraction captures only a small subset of objects in the scene.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides several semantic attributes (actions, context, communicative, transporting, age, vehicle states) but misses some ground-truth details (e.g., carrying with both hands) and includes extra unspecified track IDs.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are provided (sidewalk, on the road, near storefront), but several pedestrians that are annotated as walking on the road or jaywalking in the ground truth are labeled differently or omitted.
justifications.overall: The extraction contains useful semantic labels but is substantially incomplete and has spatial mismatches, limiting its reliability for full-scene understanding.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures only 2 pedestrians and 2 vehicles versus 7 persons and 5 vehicles in the ground truth, missing the majority of objects and failing to match track IDs.
justifications.semantic_richness: The VLM provides many attribute fields (actions, context, age, motion status) but omits or misrepresents important labels present in the ground truth such as jaywalking, specific transporting and communicative cues.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are supplied, but several appear inconsistent with the ground truth contexts (e.g., pedestrians on the road labeled as near the sidewalk) and many object-relative relations cannot be validated due to missing ID alignment.
justifications.overall: The extraction offers a moderate amount of semantic detail but is incomplete, has ID mismatches, and contains spatial/contextual inaccuracies that limit its usefulness for driving perception.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 1 of 5 pedestrians and 2 of 4 vehicles, missing most actors and many contextual annotations.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some attributes (age, communicative, motion, trunk/doors) but omits important labels like jaywalking and carrying and often uses default 'none' values.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes a few spatial relations but they are sparse and partially inconsistent with the ground truth, leaving many actors without accurate positioning.
justifications.overall: Low coverage of actors combined with partial and sometimes inaccurate attribute and spatial extraction yields limited reliability for driving perception.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures only one pedestrian and one vehicle while the ground truth contains seven persons and three vehicles, so most objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction supplies multiple semantic attributes (action, context, communicative, age, motion) but misattributes 'talking on phone' and provides incorrect or incomplete vehicle motion information and missing attributes for most objects.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial cues are provided (e.g., 'along the side of the road', 'near the center of the street') but they are sparse, not well-aligned with the ground truth objects, and many objects lack any spatial relation.
justifications.overall: The extraction shows some useful semantic detail for a single detected pedestrian and vehicle but suffers from major omissions and some incorrect labels, yielding low overall coverage and reliability.

justifications.completeness: The extraction detects only 1 of 5 pedestrians and 2 of 5 vehicles and omits or mislabels many objects and key motion states.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some attributes like age and door/trunk status are present but most atomic actions, communicative cues, and correct motion statuses are missing or incorrect.
justifications.spatial_relations: A few spatial relations are provided and partially accurate, but they are incomplete and do not match many ground truth simple contexts.
justifications.overall: The extraction is largely incomplete with limited correct semantics and partial spatial information, making it of low overall utility for the scene.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 7 ground-truth pedestrians and a single ambiguous vehicle with no matching track_ids, so most ground-truth entities are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes many attribute types (actions, contexts, transporting, motion, doors/trunk) showing good coverage per detected object, but attributes are provided for very few objects and several labels (e.g., communicative cues) are incorrect or omitted.
justifications.spatial_relations: The model supplies explicit spatial relations that partially match ground-truth context (side of road, near vehicle, parked on side) but relations are sparse and not associated with correct track_ids.
justifications.overall: Low entity recall and incorrect/missing track_id associations substantially reduce reliability despite reasonably rich attributes for the few detected objects.

justifications.completeness: Detected 5 of 11 ground-truth objects (3/6 pedestrians, 2/5 vehicles) and missed several pedestrians and vehicles including the jaywalking pedestrian and multiple vehicles, so partial coverage.
justifications.semantic_richness: Attributes are partially captured (walking, adult) but important labels like jaywalking and communicative talking in group are missed and several vehicle motion states are incorrect or absent, indicating limited semantic detail.
justifications.spatial_relations: The extraction includes some spatial cues (storefront, sidewalks, parked on left) but mislabels context for pedestrians who are on the road versus sidewalk and omits relations for several vehicles, so spatial information is inconsistent.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is partially correct but incomplete and with missing or incorrect semantic and spatial details, yielding a below-average performance.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one pedestrian out of five and two generic vehicles out of five, missing most objects and track ids.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides several attributes like actions, simple context, age, and door and trunk states but fails to capture communicative cues for at least one person and omits most instances.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes basic spatial tags such as on the sidewalk and parked along the side but misaligns with ground truth contexts for multiple pedestrians and lacks precise relative placements and track associations.
justifications.overall: Some useful attribute extraction is present but major omissions in object count, missing track ids, and incorrect or incomplete contextual labels lead to low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures all four pedestrians but only three vehicles while several ground truth vehicles are missing and track id alignment is inconsistent.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides multiple attributes like age and action but misses important labels from the ground truth such as jaywalking and a communicative 'talking in group' instance and lacks several vehicle entries.
justifications.spatial_relations: Many pedestrian spatial contexts disagree with the ground truth (e.g., walking on the road versus along the sidewalk) and some relations appear imprecise or extraneous.
justifications.overall: The output contains useful semantic fields but has substantial omissions and several incorrect spatial/contextual assignments, yielding a below average match to the ground truth.

justifications.completeness: Detected 3 of 4 pedestrians and 3 of 5 vehicles, so several objects are missing leading to partial coverage.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attribute labels (actions, age, motion) but misclassifies contexts and transporting/communicative states, producing incorrect semantics for several entities.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes pairwise spatial relations, but relations are inconsistent or unverifiable against the ground truth and are absent for many missing objects.
justifications.overall: Partially useful extraction with correct basic labels mixed with missing entities and several incorrect contextual and relational annotations, reducing overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: Detected 2 pedestrians and 1 vehicle while ground truth contains 4 persons and 4 vehicles, missing the majority of objects.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided multiple semantic attributes (actions, context, transporting, age) showing reasonable richness but included incorrect transporting labels and omitted many entities.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are present but often incorrect or too coarse (pedestrians labeled as on the sidewalk while ground truth indicates several are jaywalking on the road).
justifications.overall: Extraction supplies useful attribute types but misses most objects and contains notable spatial and attribute inaccuracies, resulting in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 4 ground-truth pedestrians and missed all vehicles and vehicle attributes.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction captured basic attributes like age and atomic action but missed key semantic labels (two jaywalking contexts) and vehicle attributes, reducing richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are provided but several are incorrect or incomplete relative to ground truth (missed jaywalking individuals on the road and mislabelled scene location as sidewalk).
justifications.overall: Extraction is partially correct for a subset of pedestrians but misses half the people, all vehicles, and important contextual labels, yielding low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected fewer pedestrians and vehicles than annotated, missing several pedestrian tracks and two vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attribute fields but several attributes are incorrect or missing compared to ground truth, offering moderate semantic detail.
justifications.spatial_relations: Many spatial relations are incorrect or overspecified, for example labeling pedestrians on sidewalks when ground truth indicates walking on the road or jaywalking.
justifications.overall: Partial detection with some useful attributes but notable omissions and several mismatches reduce reliability for autonomous driving perception.

justifications.completeness: Many ground truth actors are missing: extraction reports 4 pedestrians and 1 vehicle versus 6 pedestrians and 4 vehicles in the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction provides multiple attributes per detection (actions, context, transporting, age) but fails to capture communicative cues for some pedestrians and misstates vehicle motion status compared to ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Several pedestrians are labeled as on the sidewalk while ground truth indicates some are on the road or jaywalking and vehicle spatial context is incomplete, so spatial relations are partially incorrect.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures some attribute detail but omits multiple actors and contains several semantic and spatial errors, yielding overall poor-to-moderate quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction detects only 2 pedestrians instead of 3 and omits all vehicles from the scene, so several key entities and labels are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output includes attributes like transporting, age, and communicative status providing extra semantics, but it also contains incorrect transporting information and omits critical complex context labels such as jaywalking.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are inaccurate or imprecise compared to ground truth which indicates pedestrians are on the road jaywalking, while the extraction places them on the sidewalk ahead of the ego vehicle.
justifications.overall: Missing vehicles, incorrect spatial/contextual classifications, and at least one erroneous attribute make the extraction largely incomplete and of limited reliability for driving decisions.

justifications.completeness: Detected 2 of 6 pedestrians and none of 3 vehicles, so major omissions reduce completeness.
justifications.semantic_richness: Captures basic actions and age for two pedestrians but misses key context labels like jaywalking and all vehicle attributes.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides a couple of spatial relations but they are sparse, not aligned with the ground truth jaywalking/road locations, and many objects lack relations.
justifications.overall: Limited recall, incorrect/mismatched track coverage, and missing vehicle and contextual labels lead to poor overall extraction quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only 2 pedestrians versus 4 in the ground truth and provides two vehicles while missing or mismatching others and using unspecified track_ids, so many objects are omitted or unlinked.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes several semantic attributes (communicative, age, simple context) offering richer labels than the ground truth, but several key semantics are incorrect or inconsistent (e.g., pedestrians labeled standing/talking instead of walking).
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are reported but are often vague or incorrect relative to annotations (eg pedestrians marked as crossing near sidewalk instead of jaywalking, vehicle placements imprecise).
justifications.overall: Because of missing objects, incorrect action labels, and inaccurate spatial relations the extraction has limited reliability for driving perception despite some rich attribute reporting.

justifications.completeness: Misses multiple ground-truth pedestrians and vehicles, uses wrong/placeholder track_ids and introduces an extra bicycle, so coverage is low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attribute fields (actions, transport, age) and more semantic detail than the GT, but contains incorrect transport labels and fabricated objects, yielding moderate richness but reduced reliability.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes spatial relations for detections but several are inaccurate or inconsistent (e.g., a pedestrian annotated as both walking along the side and crossing) and it omits relations for some GT persons.
justifications.overall: Partial correctness in actions and some attributes is offset by poor object coverage, incorrect IDs, fabricated detections, and unreliable spatial information, resulting in a low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one pedestrian out of four and one vehicle out of two with incorrect track IDs, missing most entities.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extracted several attributes (action, context, age) for the single detected pedestrian but missed attributes for other people and provided an unverified vehicle class, so moderate richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided specific relations but they cannot be validated against ground truth and may be inaccurate given wrong track IDs, so partially useful.
justifications.overall: System provides some correct attribute-level information for a single pedestrian but misses most entities and has ID mismatches, yielding limited overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one of four pedestrians and one of two vehicles, so many objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides several semantic attributes (age, communicative, transporting, motion and door/trunk status) showing moderate richness but omits attributes for most true entities and some contexts.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes some spatial relations but contains inaccuracies (labels sidewalk versus road) and missing relations for other agents, reducing reliability.
justifications.overall: Extraction shows moderate semantic detail but low recall and some spatial inaccuracies, resulting in limited overall utility.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 5 ground truth pedestrians and reported vehicles with mismatched identities, so many actors are missing or misidentified.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides many attribute fields (age, transporting, trunk/doors) but several attributes are incorrect or unsupported and object types and ids do not align with ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are plausible and vehicles are roughly localized, but relations are provided for only two pedestrians and several placements are imprecise or mismatched to the ground truth.
justifications.overall: Moderate level of semantic detail is offset by low recall, identity mismatches, and several incorrect attribute assignments, yielding below average overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected 5 of 8 ground truth objects (3 of 6 pedestrians and 2 of 2 vehicles), so object recall is moderate but several pedestrians are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many per-object attributes (atomic action, context, age, communicative, transporting, vehicle trunk/door states and spatial relations) beyond the ground truth, indicating high attribute coverage despite some errors.
justifications.spatial_relations: Several spatial/context labels are incorrect or too generic compared with ground truth cues (e.g., misses two pedestrians walking on the road and mislabels a vehicle as stopped), so spatial accuracy is low.
justifications.overall: Overall performance is moderate driven by good attribute richness and vehicle detection but reduced by missed pedestrians and some incorrect spatial/motion labels.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only three pedestrians (one with a track id) while the ground truth lists seven persons with specific track ids, so many actors and associations are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output contains several attribute fields (atomic action, simple context, age) but omits or mislabels important semantics such as jaywalking and incorrectly reports the vehicle as stopped rather than moving.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some generic spatial relations are provided, but several pedestrians who are on the road in the ground truth are assigned to sidewalks and the vehicle location/motion is inconsistent with the annotation.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures basic attributes for a few actors but misses multiple actors, track associations, and key semantic and spatial details, resulting in low to moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 3 of 8 ground-truth persons and used mostly missing/incorrect track IDs, so majority of targets are not reported.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction provides many attribute fields (actions, context, age) for detected people but misses specific labels like "jaywalking" and mislabels some simple contexts.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are imprecise or incorrect for several pedestrians (none labeled as "on the road" where ground truth indicates so) and vehicle relation is generic.
justifications.overall: The model returns useful attribute structure for detected objects but misses most pedestrians, misassigns spatial/contextual details, and has incorrect tracking IDs.

justifications.completeness: Misses one person (track 0) and two vehicles (tracks 4 and 6) and mislabels the observed pedestrian action, so a substantial portion of ground truth objects and labels are omitted or incorrect
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic object types and some attributes for vehicles but fails to capture important contextual labels such as biking and jaywalking and omits several objects and motion states
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes correct relations for some parked cars and a relation to nearby construction but misses the critical relation of the pedestrian crossing the road (jaywalking) and locations for omitted vehicles
justifications.overall: Overall partially accurate for visible parked vehicles but impaired by missing objects and incorrect or missing human activity and contextual relations, yielding low to moderate reliability

justifications.completeness: The extraction omits one of the two ground truth pedestrians and only lists three vehicles versus five in the ground truth, so many objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output includes multiple attribute fields (actions, context, age, trunk and door status) indicating moderate richness, but it mislabels a pedestrian action and omits the cyclist, reducing correctness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are provided for pedestrians and vehicles, but the pedestrian relation contradicts the ground truth jaywalking label and some relative vehicle descriptions do not align with ground truth motion statuses.
justifications.overall: The extraction offers useful semantic details and several correct vehicle attributes but suffers from significant omissions and some incorrect person labeling, resulting in a below average overall assessment.

justifications.completeness: Missing several ground truth entities including one pedestrian and two vehicles while introducing an extra incorrect pedestrian, so coverage is poor.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attribute fields (age, motion, trunk/doors, spatial relations) but several attributes are incorrect or overly generic, reducing reliability.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes spatial relations for agents but some relations are incorrect or unverified against ground truth, giving only partial spatial accuracy.
justifications.overall: Partially informative extraction that contains notable omissions and inaccuracies in detection and attribute assignment, limiting its usefulness.

justifications.completeness: Misses one ground-truth pedestrian, introduces a false pedestrian (track 6 is actually a vehicle), and reports only one of three ground-truth vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides multiple attribute fields for pedestrians and vehicles but omits the critical jaywalking context and contains identity and label errors that reduce semantic fidelity.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are present but are undermined by incorrect object classifications and motion labels, making the reported relations unreliable.
justifications.overall: Some useful attributes are extracted but frequent ID mismatches, missed detections, and incorrect motion/context labels lead to low overall accuracy and reliability.

justifications.completeness: Misses one annotated person (track 0), misassigns or omits vehicle tracks and adds an extra pedestrian track, so coverage of ground truth entities is partial.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many semantic attributes (age, transporting, communicative, trunk/doors) giving rich descriptions, but some attribute labels are incorrect or unsupported by the ground truth which reduces reliability.
justifications.spatial_relations: Gives explicit spatial relations like near left sidewalk and far end of the road, but several spatial/contextual assignments contradict the ground truth (for example walking on road versus sidewalk).
justifications.overall: Supplies useful details but contains multiple identification, attribution, and spatial errors, resulting in below average overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected one of two people and two of four vehicles, missing the cyclist and several parked vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attribute fields (actions, context, age, motion, door/trunk states) but includes incorrect or assumed values for several objects.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes explicit spatial relations but some contradict the ground truth context and several objects lack precise location information.
justifications.overall: Moderate level of detail and attribute coverage but multiple missed detections and some incorrect semantic and spatial assignments limit reliability.

justifications.completeness: The extraction misses the biking/sitting person and several vehicles including a moving vehicle, so many ground-truth entities are not reported.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction supplies multiple semantic attributes (actions, age, transporting, communicative, trunk/doors) for detected entities, but several attribute assignments are incorrect or inconsistent with the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are provided (e.g., bicycle parked on the left) but key relations are incorrect or contradictory (walking reported on sidewalk vs ground truth on the road) and not all objects have relations.
justifications.overall: The extraction offers useful semantic detail for some detections but misses important entities and contains notable errors in action and spatial labeling, resulting in limited reliability.

justifications.completeness: Detected the cyclist but missed or misaligned multiple annotated vehicles and used incorrect track ids, so coverage is low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attribute fields and context but contains incorrect atomic action and implausible vehicle attributes for a bicycle, reducing semantic accuracy.
justifications.spatial_relations: Relations like 'riding a bicycle on the road' are correct and informative, though track id mismatch weakens their usefulness.
justifications.overall: Reasonable identification of a cyclist with useful spatial cues but incorrect ids, missing vehicles, and some attribute errors lead to a below-average overall result.

justifications.completeness: Missed three ground truth vehicles and only reliably matched one person while introducing an extra pedestrian, so overall coverage is low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attribute fields beyond the ground truth, but several key attributes are incorrect or mismatched such as motion status and action labels, reducing reliability.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes detailed spatial relations for some entities but several relations are incorrect or ungrounded and many objects lack spatial info, yielding partial correctness.
justifications.overall: Extraction offers useful semantic details but suffers from multiple misclassifications, missing vehicles, and incorrect motion or action labels, resulting in moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The VLM detected the cyclist and one parked car but missed multiple parked vehicles from the ground truth and used mismatched or placeholder track ids.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction contains useful labels (biking, motion status, door/trunk states and relations) but misses the pedestrian atomic action 'sitting', misattributes vehicle-level properties to a bicycle, and lacks several vehicle instances.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are clearly stated for the cyclist and the reported parked car and the rider-bike link is captured, though not all objects are localized.
justifications.overall: The output provides moderate semantic and relational information about the scene but is incomplete in object coverage and has some incorrect or missing attribute assignments.

justifications.completeness: The extraction misses most annotated vehicles and does not match the ground truth atomic action for the single person, so it captures only a small fraction of ground truth objects and attributes.
justifications.semantic_richness: The VLM provides many attribute fields (scene context, age, motion status, trunk/door states) offering rich semantics, but some key attributes are incorrect or inconsistently reported.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided spatial relations are specific and plausible for the visible cyclist and bikes, though coverage is limited and some vehicle placements are ambiguous relative to the ground truth.
justifications.overall: The extraction is moderately informative with good attribute coverage but suffers from missed objects and some incorrect attribute assignments, reducing its overall utility.

justifications.completeness: Captures the bicyclist and a couple of vehicles but misses most annotated vehicles, mismatches track IDs, and omits several ground-truth objects.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attribute types (motion, door/trunk states, age, actions) and a useful relation (ridden by), but several attributes are generic or defaulted and the atomic action is incorrect.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes reasonable spatial relations for detected entities, but relations are coarse, some are ambiguous, and they do not reliably map to ground-truth object IDs.
justifications.overall: Moderate semantic coverage with some accurate attributes but significant omissions, ID mismatches, and incomplete vehicle detection reduce overall usefulness.

justifications.completeness: Only one pedestrian and three vehicles were reported while ground truth lists one person and five vehicles, so multiple vehicles are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes many attribute fields (type, motion_status, trunk/doors) and contexts but misses the correct atomic action and has mismatched object identities.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are provided and plausible but track ID mismatches and unverifiable claims reduce confidence in their correctness.
justifications.overall: Moderate attribute detail is present but incomplete detections, incorrect atomic action, and ID inconsistencies lead to a below average overall assessment.

justifications.completeness: Many ground-truth pedestrians (ids 0,4,6) and all vehicles are missing while several false positive pedestrian tracks are reported, so the extraction is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: The VLM provides multiple attribute fields (age, transporting, simple contexts) and correctly identifies some attributes but misses key semantics like pushing and looking into phone and mislabels several transporting and context states.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial cues are present (sidewalk, crosswalk) but many are assigned to wrong or spurious track ids and lack correspondence with ground truth positions, reducing reliability.
justifications.overall: The extraction contains useful attribute structure but suffers from missing objects, incorrect track assignments, and omitted vehicles, resulting in limited overall accuracy.

justifications.completeness: Misses several ground-truth pedestrians and all vehicles, reporting only six pedestrians with several incorrect or unmatched IDs.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides a wide set of attribute types (actions, context, communicative, transporting, age) but many attributes are incorrect, misassigned, or inconsistent with the ground truth and vehicles are omitted.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes spatial relations but several are inaccurate or inconsistent with ground truth (mislabeling crosswalk vs jaywalking and an unexpected directing traffic tag).
justifications.overall: Extraction captures useful attribute categories but suffers from missing objects, ID mismatches and multiple semantic and spatial errors, limiting its reliability for driving perception.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only three pedestrians plus a traffic officer and no vehicles, missing at least three annotated persons and two parked vehicles from the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output includes actions, transporting and age attributes and a scene label, but it fails to capture several important context labels such as jaywalking, looking into phone, pushing, and vehicle states.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some pairwise relations and proximity cues are provided, however track id usage is inconsistent with ground truth and some relational context like crosswalk versus jaywalking is incorrect.
justifications.overall: The extraction contains useful semantic elements but is substantially incomplete, includes ID and context mismatches, and omits vehicles, making it only partially reliable for downstream use.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports five pedestrians versus seven in the ground truth and mismatches several identities, so several people are missed or incorrectly matched.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output contains many semantic attributes (actions, transporting, age, spatial relations) providing rich descriptions, but some attributes are incorrect or unsupported by the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are frequently provided but include inaccurate or unverified relations (for example a traffic officer directing traffic and several side placements that conflict with annotations).
justifications.overall: The extraction is moderately informative with rich semantics but suffers from missing pedestrians, identity mismatches, and several incorrect attribute and spatial assignments, resulting in average overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 3 pedestrians and 1 vehicle versus 7 persons and 3 vehicles in the ground truth, missing most actors and using mismatched track_ids.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides multiple semantic attributes (transporting, age, communicative, spatial relations) and some correct facts (e.g., carrying with both hands) but also contains incorrect age labels and missing/incorrect attributes for several actors.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes explicit spatial relations, but many relations are unsupported by the ground truth and some use incorrect track identifiers, reducing trustworthiness.
justifications.overall: Captures a few correct attributes but misses most actors and contains ID and attribute errors, resulting in low overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 3 of 7 pedestrians and missed several specific contexts and actions, though both vehicles were reported, yielding limited coverage.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction contains many semantic fields (atomic_action, simple_context, communicative, transporting, age) providing rich descriptions despite some inaccuracies and missing categories like biking and standing.
justifications.spatial_relations: Several pedestrian spatial relations are incorrect or omitted (ground truth shows multiple people on the road and crossing) while the output overuses sidewalk/background relations.
justifications.overall: Moderate overall performance driven by good semantic variety and vehicle reporting but undermined by poor pedestrian recall and spatial/contextual inaccuracies.

justifications.completeness: Detected far fewer pedestrians and vehicles than present in the ground truth, missing most persons and some vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided a broad set of semantic attributes (actions, context, communicative, transporting, age) and captured some correct details like 'looking into phone' and 'carrying with both hands' but missed or misassigned several attributes and track IDs.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are coarse and often incorrect (e.g., labeling many subjects as on the sidewalk vs on the road) and use vague positional phrases that reduce spatial accuracy.
justifications.overall: The extraction offers useful semantic attributes but is incomplete and contains notable spatial and instance-matching errors, yielding limited reliability for perception tasks.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only one pedestrian and one vehicle while the ground truth contains six people and four vehicles, so most objects are missing.

justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are given (standing in the middle of the road, stopped in the background) but many positional relations for other pedestrians and vehicles are absent or imprecise.
justifications.overall: The extraction contains a few correct elements but is largely incomplete and lacks the detailed semantic and spatial coverage of the ground truth.

justifications.completeness: The extraction detected only 3 pedestrians out of 7 and 2 vehicles out of 4, missing a majority of ground-truth agents.

justifications.overall: The output includes useful schema and some correct labels but is incomplete, contains multiple incorrect spatial and action assignments, and fails to match most ground-truth tracks.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 pedestrians and 2 vehicles while ground truth lists 7 persons and 4 vehicles, missing the majority of entities.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only 3 pedestrians and 1 vehicle versus 8 pedestrians and 4 vehicles in the ground truth, missing the majority of agents.

justifications.overall: Overall performance is low due to many missed detections and incomplete or partially incorrect semantic and spatial annotations.

justifications.completeness: Many ground-truth people and vehicles are missing or unmatched and important attributes like transporting (pushing) and bicyclist are omitted.

justifications.completeness: Many annotated pedestrians and all vehicles from the ground truth are missing or have incorrect track IDs, so coverage is very low.

justifications.overall: Overall the output is incomplete and partially inaccurate, offering limited reliable semantic information compared to the ground truth.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 5 pedestrians and 2 of 4 vehicles, missing several objects and transport actions.

justifications.spatial_relations: Provided some spatial descriptors (for example, on the sidewalk) but many relations are vague, incomplete, or not properly associated with GT object identities.

justifications.completeness: Detected only two pedestrians and two vehicles while ground truth lists five persons and four vehicles, missing several objects and track associations.

justifications.overall: Extraction provides modest semantic detail but is incomplete and contains spatial and association errors, yielding low overall usefulness for driving perception.

justifications.completeness: Only 2 of 4 annotated pedestrians and 2 of 3 annotated vehicles are represented and several actors (including a person getting on a 2-wheel and a pedestrian pushing) are missing or merged, so completeness is low.

justifications.overall: The output is semantically rich but suffers from missing entities and several incorrect spatial/role attributions, resulting in an overall low to moderate quality extraction.

justifications.completeness: Detected only two of four pedestrians and two of three vehicles, missing key actors and behaviors such as pushing and getting on a two-wheeler.

justifications.overall: Partially accurate extraction with correct basic detections but missing actors and key semantic details, resulting in moderate overall usefulness.

justifications.completeness: The extraction only reports two pedestrians and two vehicles while the ground truth lists five persons and three vehicles, missing multiple actors and some key attributes like transporting for persons that are pushing.

justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations match plausible elements such as sidewalk and near construction barriers, but several relations are coarse, partially incorrect, and not provided for many ground truth objects.

justifications.completeness: Detected three of seven pedestrians and the single vehicle (4 of 8 ground-truth objects), missing several people including a cyclist and a jaywalking pedestrian.

justifications.spatial_relations: Includes useful spatial relations (near barriers, carts, parked vehicle) that align with scene elements and capture object interactions, though some relations are imprecise and not exhaustive.
justifications.overall: Partially complete and semantically informative with reasonable spatial cues, but misses multiple pedestrians and contains some attribute errors, yielding moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 3 of 6 ground-truth pedestrians and 1 of 4 vehicles, missing several actors.

justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are sparse and partly incorrect or ambiguous, with no accurate linking to ground-truth track ids and an erroneous vehicle relation.
justifications.overall: Captures some high-level attributes but misses the majority of actors and includes incorrect relational and transporting labels, resulting in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected 2 of 10 annotated pedestrians and 1 vehicle versus 5 annotated vehicles, missing most actors.

justifications.overall: Overall extraction is sparse and partially incorrect, with low recall of entities and mixed attribute accuracy.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only 3 pedestrians and 1 vehicle compared with 9 pedestrians and 4 vehicles in the ground truth, missing the majority of objects.

justifications.spatial_relations: Some useful spatial cues are present (sides of the road, near barriers/crates) but the relations are sparse, inconsistent with many GT instances, and lack coverage for most persons and vehicles.
justifications.overall: The extraction contains some accurate per-instance semantics but has low recall of objects and incomplete contextual and spatial information, yielding limited utility for full-scene understanding.

justifications.completeness: Only 2 pedestrians and 1 vehicle were reported versus 7 pedestrians and 5 vehicles in the ground truth, missing the majority of objects.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes many attribute fields (actions, simple context, age, doors/trunk) but several attributes are incorrect or mismatched and many objects lack any semantics.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations (sidewalk, parked on the side of the road) match parts of the scene, but several pedestrian locations and relations are incorrect or omitted.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures a few correct details for a small number of objects but misses most entities and contains notable inaccuracies, resulting in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected far fewer pedestrians and vehicles than present, missing several ground-truth persons and two moving vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attribute labels and scene context but several key semantic labels are incorrect or not aligned with ground truth, reducing trustworthiness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes useful spatial relations for some entities and a correctly labeled parked car but also contains incorrect relations and omits many on-road positions.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is incomplete and contains multiple semantic and spatial errors despite some correct labels, resulting in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction lists six pedestrians and a bicycle but fails to label two persons as biking (sitting) from the ground truth and instead reports a separate bicycle vehicle, so key semantic entities and actions are partially missing or misassigned.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output provides many attributes (atomic_action, simple_context, transporting, age, vehicle motion) showing richness, but several attribute values are incorrect or irrelevant (e.g., marking a person as carrying when ground truth has no transporting and treating cyclists as a bicycle with trunk/doors).
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are present and sometimes useful (near bicycle, near construction barriers), but relations are occasionally misassigned to vehicle or person classes and do not correct the missing person-biking associations from the ground truth.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures many entities and descriptive fields but contains notable misclassifications (cyclists vs bicycle), some incorrect attribute values, and partially inaccurate spatial assignments, yielding moderate but insufficient performance.

justifications.completeness: The extraction lists four pedestrians versus five in the ground truth and uses unlinked track IDs for vehicles, so multiple people are missing or not matched.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output provides many semantic attributes (actions, transporting, age, spatial relations) beyond the ground truth, but several attribute values are incorrect or inconsistent with the annotations.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are specified for several entities and reference relevant objects like traffic cones, but some relations are vague or do not align with the ground-truth object placements and IDs.
justifications.overall: The extraction is informative and semantically rich but has significant errors in track matching, missing persons, and incorrect attribute assignments that reduce overall usefulness.

justifications.completeness: Missing several ground-truth persons (IDs 2 and 5) and most GT vehicles are omitted or misidentified, so coverage is limited.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attribute fields (actions, age, spatial_relations) but contains systematic errors (many walkers instead of bikers, all ages as adult) reducing semantic correctness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some useful relations are present (sidewalk, near road, near cones) but several relations are imprecise or incorrect and vehicle-to-person associations are wrong.

justifications.completeness: The extraction detects only 2 of 7 ground-truth pedestrians and 2 of 5 ground-truth vehicles, missing most objects and including an extra misclassified person.
justifications.semantic_richness: The model provides many attribute labels (actions, age, transporting, motion status) but several are incorrect or misassigned (e.g., wrong age for track 5 and transporting assigned to a non-matching track).
justifications.spatial_relations: Some plausible spatial relations are provided (sides of road, near barriers, parked locations) but many relations are missing, unverified against ground truth, or incorrectly attributed to wrong tracks.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures basic attributes and some spatial info but suffers from low recall, misassignments, and incomplete coverage leading to moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Many ground-truth persons and vehicles are missing or unmatched, with only two pedestrians and two unlabeled vehicles reported.

justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are provided, but several are inaccurate or imprecise and there is no reliable mapping to ground-truth object IDs or many vehicle locations.
justifications.overall: The output offers useful semantic attributes but suffers from poor coverage and imperfect spatial grounding, limiting its reliability for driving scene understanding.

justifications.completeness: Only 3 pedestrians were reported versus 7 in the ground truth and only 1 vehicle versus 2, with missing track IDs and omitted persons, so the extraction is largely incomplete.

justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are provided but several are incorrect or invented (e.g., labeling road walkers as on the sidewalk and mentioning construction cones not present in the ground truth), so spatial accuracy is poor.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures one vehicle and three pedestrians but misses four ground-truth persons and several important attributes, so coverage is limited.

justifications.overall: The extraction provides a partial and somewhat useful summary but suffers from missing entities, incorrect context assignments, and limited attribute detail, yielding a below‑average result.

justifications.completeness: Detected four of six ground truth entities (three pedestrians and one vehicle), missing two pedestrians including a standing person and a person carrying items.

justifications.overall: Partial detection and attribute extraction with several missed entities and some incorrect or inconsistent labels lead to moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected three pedestrians and one vehicle but failed to match track ids and mislabelled one pedestrian's action and the senior age, so several ground truth attributes are missing or incorrect.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attributes beyond the ground truth such as transporting, communicative, and explicit spatial relations, but some of those added attributes are incorrect (eg carrying and ages).
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes spatial relations but several are inconsistent with the annotations, for example a person walking on the road was labelled as on the sidewalk and the standing person was not recognised.
justifications.overall: Moderate overall coverage and useful extra details, but errors in identities, actions, ages, and spatial relations reduce the extraction's reliability for safety-critical use.

justifications.completeness: Most ground-truth pedestrians are missing or have incorrect track IDs and the single vehicle is mismatched, with only one transporting attribute (carrying with both hands) captured.

justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are provided but several are unverified or incorrect relative to ground truth (eg near traffic cones, walking away), lowering accuracy.
justifications.overall: The output provides useful semantic detail but is substantially incomplete and contains identity and spatial inaccuracies that limit its reliability for perception tasks.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures only 3 of the 7 ground-truth pedestrians and the vehicle, missing multiple people and failing to preserve correct track IDs.

justifications.spatial_relations: Some useful spatial relations are reported such as near a parked truck and ahead/behind, but they are inconsistently anchored with wrong track IDs and do not cover most pedestrians.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction gives useful detail for a subset of entities but low recall, ID errors, and some incorrect attributes limit its reliability for driving perception.

justifications.completeness: Only one of seven ground-truth pedestrians was reported and several attributes from GT are missing, though the vehicle was roughly captured.

justifications.completeness: Only two pedestrians were reported out of six annotated, missing four detections and failing to match track IDs.

justifications.overall: Extraction captures some semantic detail for a subset of actors but has low coverage and errors in attributes, yielding an overall limited performance.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only 2 pedestrians while the ground truth lists 5 and omits several actors and their key attributes, though it correctly notes no vehicles.

justifications.completeness: Detected 1 pedestrian out of 6 annotated persons and no vehicles, so most entities are missing.

justifications.completeness: Detected 2 pedestrians compared to 6 ground-truth persons and track IDs do not match, so many actors are missed.

justifications.completeness: Detected 2 of 5 ground-truth pedestrians (low recall), missing three walking pedestrians and thus incomplete coverage.

justifications.completeness: Only two pedestrians are reported while six are present in the ground truth and both vehicles are omitted, so the extraction is largely incomplete.

justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are provided but they are partially unmatched or potentially incorrect relative to ground truth and many actors lack any spatial information.
justifications.overall: The extraction contains some useful per-person attributes but misses the majority of actors and all vehicles, resulting in poor overall performance.

justifications.completeness: Only one pedestrian was reported with a nonmatching track id while four annotated persons and all vehicles were omitted.

justifications.completeness: Misses multiple ground truth persons and most parked vehicles, providing only four pedestrians and a single bicycle instead of five people and many parked vehicles.

justifications.overall: Partial and somewhat detailed extraction that nevertheless misses key objects and contains several incorrect attribute assignments, limiting its reliability.

justifications.completeness: Detected four pedestrians matching the ground truth count but failed to match track IDs and omitted multiple parked vehicles, so coverage is partial.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many semantic fields (communicative, transporting, age, context) beyond the bare GT but several attribute values contradict the annotations or are incorrect.

justifications.completeness: Detected two of four ground truth pedestrians and missed two, while also producing a false positive vehicle.

justifications.completeness: Detected both pedestrians from the ground truth but used different track IDs and added a false positive vehicle, so entity coverage is only partial.

justifications.completeness: Detected the single pedestrian and vehicle from the ground truth and even an extra vehicle, but misidentified the pedestrian's action, used a different transport verb, and mismatched track IDs, so it is only partially complete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many semantic attributes (actions, transport, age, vehicle states, spatial relations) that exceed the ground truth in richness, although several key attributes are incorrect or inconsistent with the annotations.

justifications.completeness: Both ground truth pedestrians are represented but one pedestrian action and transport label are incorrect and the single ground truth vehicle is not clearly matched while an extra vehicle was added.

justifications.spatial_relations: Several spatial relations are provided and one vehicle is described as parked which aligns with ground truth, but other spatial claims are unverified or inconsistent with annotations.
justifications.overall: The output is detailed and partially matches the scene but contains notable semantic errors (wrong actions, transport labels, ages), an extra vehicle, and inconsistent track associations, reducing reliability.

justifications.completeness: The extraction misses the single pedestrian and fails to report several ground-truth vehicles (IDs 4,5,7) while adding an incorrect vehicle (ID 3), so coverage is low.
justifications.semantic_richness: The VLM provides several attributes (motion status, trunk/doors, and descriptive spatial phrases) for detected vehicles but omits person attributes entirely and introduces some unsupported details.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations appear plausible for reported vehicles, but relations include errors tied to a falsely detected vehicle and many vehicles lack any spatial description.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures basic vehicle attributes for a subset of objects and gives extra contextual phrases, but it misses the pedestrian, omits multiple ground-truth vehicles, and includes at least one incorrect object, yielding modest overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 8 ground-truth objects (vehicles 6 and 7) and missed the pedestrian plus five other vehicles, yielding low completeness.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction reports many semantic attributes (type, motion_status, trunk/doors, spatial_relations) for each detected object, but several reported objects are false positives or unverifiable against the ground truth, so richness is moderate.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are provided for every extracted vehicle and are specific, but many relations cannot be validated with the provided ground truth and some apply to false positives, so partial credit is warranted.
justifications.overall: Low recall and presence of false positives despite reasonable attribute coverage result in an overall low-to-moderate quality extraction.

justifications.completeness: The extraction recovers only four of the seven ground-truth vehicles and misses the single ground-truth pedestrian while introducing a spurious vehicle, so recall of annotated entities is about half.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output provides useful attributes like motion, trunk and door states and object types, but it asserts closed states where ground truth is unknown for some objects and fails to capture the pedestrian's action and presence.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are provided and plausible for some vehicles but are undermined by misassociated track ids, missing objects and unverifiable positional claims relative to the limited ground-truth.
justifications.overall: The extraction contains useful semantic detail for detected vehicles but misses critical entities, mislabels one object class, and makes unverified attribute assertions, yielding an overall middling performance.

justifications.completeness: Detected 4 of 8 ground truth objects (vehicles 1,2,4,6) but missed the pedestrian and vehicles 0,5,7 and introduced a false vehicle for track 3.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided detailed per-vehicle attributes like motion_status, trunk_open, and doors_open, but misclassified a person as a truck and omitted pedestrian attributes.
justifications.spatial_relations: Included plausible spatial relations for detected vehicles, but relations are absent for missed objects and may be incorrect for the misclassified track.
justifications.overall: Moderate performance: reasonable attribute extraction for detected vehicles but failures in detection, a critical misclassification of a person, and incomplete spatial coverage reduce reliability.

justifications.completeness: Misses one of two pedestrians and one of two vehicles and uses an invalid track id, so detection is incomplete.

justifications.spatial_relations: Adds specific spatial relations that are plausible but are not present in the ground truth and cannot be verified here, so their correctness is uncertain.
justifications.overall: Partially correct attributes and actions for one pedestrian and one vehicle but omissions, track id errors, and unverifiable spatial claims yield a low to moderate score.

justifications.completeness: Detected two vehicles but missed two pedestrians and one ground-truth vehicle, so overall object coverage is low.

justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are well captured (sitting on the side, near a fence, riding on the road, bicycle linked to Pedestrian #2) and align with the scene layout.
justifications.overall: Major entities and relations are extracted correctly but critical attribute errors (atomic actions and ages) limit the extraction's reliability for downstream use.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one pedestrian and a bicycle while ground truth contains two persons and one moving vehicle, and key attributes like sitting are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attribute fields (action, transporting, age, spatial relations) but several values conflict with ground truth such as action and age, reducing reliability.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes relations like under a tunnel and being pulled that are not supported by ground truth and may be incorrect, so spatial relation accuracy is low.
justifications.overall: Extraction contains some detailed attributes but is largely incorrect and incomplete compared to ground truth, yielding a low overall score.

justifications.completeness: The extraction found the single person and matched most categorical fields (action, contexts) and vehicles, but failed to correctly label the person's age (senior vs adult).
justifications.semantic_richness: The output contains multiple semantic attributes (action, contexts, spatial relation, scene context) providing rich information beyond the ground truth, though one attribute (age) is incorrect.
justifications.spatial_relations: A specific spatial relation ('sitting against the wall of the underpass') is provided and is consistent with the scene context, but the ground truth lacks spatial labels so this cannot be fully verified.
justifications.overall: The extraction is largely accurate for presence and action and adds useful context, but the incorrect age label reduces overall reliability to a moderate-high score.

justifications.completeness: Detected both the person and the bicycle but missed the person's atomic action, misclassified age as adult instead of senior, and used inconsistent track IDs, so important labels are incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attribute fields (context, communicative, transporting, spatial relations) but leaves atomic action unspecified and unnecessarily asserts trunk/door states for a bicycle, reducing reliable semantic detail.
justifications.spatial_relations: Accurately captures key spatial relations such as on a bicycle, under a tunnel, and being ridden by the pedestrian, which align well with the ground truth context.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is useful for spatial and motion understanding but contains notable errors in atomic action, age classification, and some attribute assignments that limit full correctness.

justifications.completeness: Detects both people and the bicycle and reports motion, but misses or mislabels key attributes such as both subjects' ages and one person's atomic action and transporting status.
justifications.semantic_richness: Includes many semantic attributes and relations beyond the ground truth (e.g., transporting, basket, vehicle door/trunk states), but several of these are incorrect or unsupported, reducing trustworthiness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Correctly indicates that persons are on/riding the bicycle and links occupants to the bike, but introduces an incorrect child/adult relation and omits the correct aging/contextual labels, so relations are only partly accurate.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction captures the main entities and some relations, but significant errors in age classification and transporting/atomic actions limit its overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: The extraction identifies the person and most behavioral attributes but uses a non-matching track_id and misclassifies age, reducing completeness.
justifications.semantic_richness: The VLM provides additional scene context and spatial relations beyond the ground truth, though it incorrectly labels the pedestrian as an adult instead of senior.
justifications.spatial_relations: The reported spatial relations accurately describe the subject sitting against a wall in an underpass, which matches the scene description.
justifications.overall: Extraction is mostly correct on action and context but age misclassification and track_id mismatch lower the overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: The extraction correctly detects the single person and fills most attributes but mislabels age and uses a different track id, so it is largely complete but not fully accurate.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output provides scene description and contextual labels but misses the correct age category and could include more precise attributes, reducing semantic fidelity.
justifications.spatial_relations: The extraction gives a clear and plausible spatial relation (sitting against the right wall) that aligns well with the scene context, so spatial detail is strong.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is good at detecting and describing the person and scene but the age misclassification and minor ID mismatch reduce its overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: Captures the person, correct sitting action, and simple context but mislabels age, adds an incorrect communicative attribute, and uses a different track id.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides additional attributes and spatial details beyond the ground truth but includes incorrect communicative and age labels which reduce reliability.
justifications.spatial_relations: Offers plausible and relevant spatial relations that align with the scene description even though the ground truth lacks explicit spatial annotations.
justifications.overall: Accurate for the main action and context but weakened by incorrect age and communicative labels, resulting in moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Extraction contains the single person and most required attributes but mislabels the age and uses a different track_id, so it is not fully complete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides extra scene description and contextual detail beyond the ground truth, but loses points for the incorrect age classification and a generic location tag.
justifications.spatial_relations: Accurately and clearly describes the person as sitting against the right wall of the tunnel, matching the visual context.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is largely accurate and informative with correct actions and spatial relations, but the incorrect age label and minor ID/location mismatches reduce the overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction covers the same person and includes most annotated attributes and additional fields, though it does not explicitly list complex contextual as a separate field.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides extra semantic detail such as spatial relations but contains incorrect attribute labels for age and communicative state, reducing its semantic accuracy.
justifications.spatial_relations: The extraction gives a specific and plausible spatial relation consistent with the scene context (underpass/tunnel), even though the ground truth did not annotate spatial relations.
justifications.overall: While the key action and context are detected, incorrect claims about age and a false communicative inference significantly lower overall reliability.
